game.name: Sokoban
game.history: The Sokoban puzzle was originally conceived by Hiroyuki Imabayashi, founder of Thinking Rabbit, a video game developer. It was released commercially in 1982.
game.board: This board consists of a grid-based warehouse layout containing a worker, crates, walls, and designated target locations.
game.pieces: There are an equal number of crates and targets, which can be moved by the worker.
game.tomove: Click an arrow to move the worker into an adjacent empty tile, or into a crate to push it one step in the same direction.
game.towin: Move every crate to a designated storage location (target) on the board.
game.rules: The worker can only move up, down, left, or right into an empty space. The worker can push a single crate, but cannot pull crates. Crates cannot be pushed into walls or other crates. The puzzle is solved when each crate is located on a target.

strategy.name: Deadlock Avoidance
strategy.description: Identify deadlock spaces — squares where a crate can never be pushed out, such as corners or narrow corridors — and avoid pushing crates into those positions. Plan moves in advance rather than reacting one step at a time.

strategy.name: Goal-Oriented Pushing
strategy.description: Keep the worker behind the crates and push them toward the goals rather than moving them sideways unnecessarily. Work one crate at a time where possible, and avoid blocking the paths needed for remaining crates.

references.reference: "Sokoban." Wikipedia, Wikimedia Foundation, en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sokoban. Accessed 27 May 2026.

gamescrafters.gamescrafter: Doris Chiang (Backend, GamesmanUni GUI Design & Implementation)
Kshitij Tomar (Backend, GamesmanUni GUI Implementation, Variants)
